intro: Patagonium prostratum, also known as Chilean fire vine, is a climbing plant native to the temperate rainforests of South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its use in treating conditions like arthritis and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of Patagonium prostratum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties.
